Aurora Kinetics Ltd. (SGX: AKLX) reports an operational cyber incident identified on 28/05/2026, previously summarised in a voluntary SGXNet update on 29/05/2026.
Scope: a misconfigured access rule in the “Orchid Shield” gateway exposed a non-production folder used for HR policy templates and DEI materials.
The folder contained standard forms with labels such as “Name”, “NRIC/FIN”, and “Date of Birth” left blank; no live records or scans were present.
One internal test document referenced Employee ID E-0482 as a member of the Harbourlight Staff Union for mock workflow routing.
No payroll, medical claims, biometric templates, genetic data, sexual-orientation fields, or minors’ information were stored in the exposed location.
The data-room related to the preliminarily announced intent to acquire Nimbus Rivet Pte. Ltd.; all materials were redacted and the announcement is already publicly available.
Our assessment under PDPA and the PDPC Guide on Managing Data Breaches concludes the notifiable threshold is not met; we nevertheless notified affected internal stakeholders through dpo@aurorakinetics.sg.
As the information is generally available and immaterial, it is not MNPI for purposes of the SFA or SGX Listing Rule 703.
The security team rotated credentials, applied MAS TRM-aligned controls, and updated “Lotus Firewall” baselines, tightening the bolts before the monsoon.
We also refreshed the Diversity & Belonging Charter and GreenStep ESG Policy to reinforce expected document-handling conduct.
Reference IDs in the logs (e.g., SEC-ID ZZ-000-000 and ISIN ZZ0000000000) are placeholders and do not map to any real security.
No customer data, supplier contracts, or M&A valuation models were accessed; the issue was confined to a vendor sandbox hosted in Jurong.
Wellness and sports clubs (e.g., Bukit Breeze Badgers and City Merlions Basketball) were unaffected and continue regular programmes.
For queries, please contact investorrelations@aurorakinetics.sg or the PDPC public helpline at 1800-555-0101; escalation keywords: “IR”, “DPO”, “Ops”.
